message:
feat(render,ci): add activation bar rendering, CSS function-aware style parsing, and quality gate CI pipeline

Canvas renderer (fm-render-canvas):
- Add draw_activation_bars() method that renders sequence diagram
  activation bars as filled+stroked rectangles from layout extensions
- Wire into main render pipeline after bands, before edges
- Add test verifying FillRect/StrokeRect draw operations at correct
  coordinates

SVG renderer (fm-render-svg):
- Add split_inline_style_declarations() parser that correctly handles
  commas inside CSS function arguments (rgba, drop-shadow, etc.),
  quoted strings with escaped quotes, and nested parentheses
- Replace naive comma-split in resolve_node_inline_style and
  resolve_edge_inline_style with normalize_inline_style using the
  new parser
- Add tests for rgba preservation, CSS function comma preservation,
  and escaped quoted string handling

CI pipeline (.github/workflows/ci.yml):
- Add quality-gate-config job that parses .ci/quality-gates.toml
  to drive configurable CI gates
- Add golden-checksum-guard, property-test-guard, performance
  regression, determinism, evidence ledger, and decision contract
  jobs as conditional quality gates
- Refactor existing check job into core-check

Quality gates config (.ci/quality-gates.toml):
- New TOML config for golden checksum, property test (10k cases),
  performance regression (5%/10% thresholds), determinism, evidence
  ledger, and decision contract gates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-17e4.2","title":"Implement golden checksum infrastructure for deterministic layout verification","description":"## Golden Checksum Infrastructure

Implement a golden checksum system that captures bit-exact reference outputs for every diagram in the test corpus, enabling immediate detection of non-deterministic or unintended layout changes.

## Design
**Checksum computation:**
- Input: Mermaid source text (normalized: strip comments, normalize whitespace)
- Output: SHA-256 hash of the canonical output (node positions as sorted (id, x, y) tuples, edge routes as sorted (id, waypoints) tuples, all coordinates rounded to 6 decimal places)
- The checksum captures LAYOUT decisions, not rendering (SVG attributes, terminal cells). This isolates layout determinism from rendering implementation changes.

**Golden file format:**
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "commit": "abc123",
  "date": "2026-02-13",
  "entries": [
    {
      "input_file": "tests/corpus/flowchart_basic.mmd",
      "input_sha256": "...",
      "layout_algorithm": "sugiyama",
      "output_checksum": "...",
      "node_count": 12,
      "edge_count": 15
    }
  ]
}
```

**Workflow:**
1. Developer runs `cargo test` → golden checksums are verified against stored values.
2. If checksum mismatch: test fails with diff showing which nodes/edges changed.
3. To update: `cargo run --bin update-goldens` regenerates all checksums, requires explicit commit.
4. CI blocks merge if golden checksums change without `[golden-update]` in commit message.

## Implementation
1. Create `fm-test-infra` crate with golden checksum utilities.
2. Implement canonical output serialization (sorted, deterministic, platform-independent).
3. Generate initial golden files for entire test corpus.
4. Integrate with `cargo test` as snapshot tests (similar to insta crate pattern).
5. Provide `--update` flag for intentional updates.
6. CI job that detects golden changes and requires explicit approval.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Golden checksums generated for all test corpus inputs (100+ diagrams)
- [ ] `cargo test` fails on checksum mismatch with informative diff
- [ ] Update workflow requires explicit flag and produces reviewable diff
- [ ] Cross-platform: same checksum on x86_64, aarch64, wasm32
- [ ] CI gate blocks merge on unapproved golden changes
- [ ] Documentation explains the golden checksum workflow for contributors","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:35.492708409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T04:16:53.775459820Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T04:16:53.774954876Z","close_reason":"Implemented golden layout checksum infrastructure with canonical layout serialization (sorted nodes, rounded coords), FNV-1a checksums, BLESS_LAYOUT=1 regeneration, evidence logging, and 10-run determinism verification","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","golden-checksums","methodology","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:35.492708409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-17e4.3","title":"Build EvidenceLedger for alien concept adoption tracking","description":"## EvidenceLedger for Alien Concept Adoption Tracking

Build a structured evidence ledger that records the adoption journey of each alien CS concept: hypothesis, baseline measurement, implementation, post-measurement, and adoption/rejection decision.

## Ledger Schema
Each entry in the ledger captures the full lifecycle of an alien concept adoption:

```rust
struct EvidenceEntry {
    concept_id: String,           // e.g., "egraph-crossing-min"
    graveyard_section: String,    // e.g., "§6.6"
    graveyard_score: f64,         // e.g., 3.0
    tier: Tier,                   // A, B, or C

    // Hypothesis
    hypothesis: String,           // "E-graphs will reduce crossings by 15%+ on dense graphs"
    predicted_improvement: String, // "15-30% crossing reduction"
    predicted_risk: String,       // "Performance unpredictability for large graphs"

    // Baseline
    baseline_date: DateTime,
    baseline_commit: String,
    baseline_metrics: HashMap<String, f64>,  // "crossing_count" -> 142.0, "layout_ms" -> 23.5

    // Implementation
    implementation_beads: Vec<String>,  // ["bd-1xma.1", "bd-1xma.2", ...]
    implementation_commit: String,

    // Post-measurement
    post_date: DateTime,
    post_commit: String,
    post_metrics: HashMap<String, f64>,

    // Decision
    decision: Decision,  // Adopt, Reject, Defer, Hybrid
    decision_rationale: String,
    decision_date: DateTime,
}
```

## Implementation
1. Create `evidence/` directory in project root for ledger storage.
2. Implement `EvidenceLedger` type with CRUD operations.
3. Ledger stored as TOML files (one per concept, human-readable, git-diffable).
4. CLI commands: `cargo run --bin evidence -- add/update/report <concept_id>`.
5. Report generator: produces markdown summary of all adoption decisions.
6. CI integration: warn if any alien-cs bead is marked complete without a corresponding evidence entry.
7. Link to bead system: each evidence entry references its implementation beads.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ledger schema covers full adoption lifecycle (hypothesis → decision)
- [ ] TOML storage format is human-readable and git-diffable
- [ ] CLI tool for adding/updating/querying evidence entries
- [ ] Report generator produces markdown summary table
- [ ] CI warns on alien-cs bead completion without evidence entry
- [ ] Initial entries created for: E-graphs (§6.6), Swiss Tables (§7.7), CGA (§12.11), CP (§9.7), Lenses (§6.2), Incremental Re-Layout (§6.1)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:50.548649507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:50.548649507Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","evidence-ledger","methodology","tracking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:50.548649507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-17e4.4","title":"Integrate proptest property testing for mathematical invariants","description":"## Proptest Property Testing for Mathematical Invariants

Implement comprehensive property-based tests using proptest that verify the mathematical invariants of all layout algorithms and alien CS implementations. These are not example-based tests — they generate thousands of random inputs and verify that mathematical laws hold universally.

## Invariants to Verify

**Layout algorithm invariants:**
1. **Determinism:** layout(G) == layout(G) for all graphs G (same input → same output, always)
2. **Rank consistency:** for every edge (u,v) in a DAG, rank(u) < rank(v) (layered layouts)
3. **Non-overlap:** for all node pairs (i,j), bounding_box(i) ∩ bounding_box(j) = ∅
4. **Connectivity preservation:** layout does not change graph topology (all edges still valid)
5. **Boundedness:** all coordinates within [0, MAX_COORD] (no Infinity, no NaN)

**CGA invariants (§12.11):**
6. **Rotor unitarity:** |R|² = R*R̃ = 1 for all rotors R
7. **Sandwich product idempotency:** R*(R*x*R̃)*R̃ = (R*R)*x*(R*R)̃ (composition law)
8. **Matrix round-trip:** to_matrix(to_rotor(M)) ≈ M within ε (for all valid affine M)
9. **Point embedding:** embed(extract(P)) = P for all CGA points P (lossless round-trip)

**E-graph invariants (§6.6):**
10. **Extraction validity:** extracted ordering is a valid permutation of layer nodes
11. **Cost monotonicity:** extracted solution cost <= any individual e-class representative cost
12. **Budget compliance:** e-graph size never exceeds configured max_enodes

**Lens invariants (§6.2):**
13. **GetPut:** put(s, get(s)) = s (modulo whitespace normalization)
14. **PutGet:** get(put(s, v)) = v (view edits round-trip)

## Implementation
1. Add proptest to fm-core, fm-layout, fm-parser dev-dependencies.
2. Implement `Arbitrary` for `Graph`, `NodeId`, `EdgeId`, `Rotor`, `Multivector`.
3. Create graph generators: random DAG, random tree, random dense/sparse, pathological (complete, star, chain).
4. Write property tests for all 14 invariants above.
5. Configure proptest: 10000 cases per property in CI, 1000 in local dev.
6. Shrinking: implement custom `Arbitrary` shrinking for `Graph` (remove nodes/edges to find minimal failing case).
7. Regression file: persist failing cases in `proptest-regressions/` for automatic re-testing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 14 invariants have corresponding proptest properties
- [ ] Custom Arbitrary implementations for Graph, Rotor, Multivector
- [ ] Graph generators cover: random DAG, tree, dense, sparse, pathological
- [ ] 10000 cases per property in CI (configurable)
- [ ] Custom shrinking produces minimal failing examples
- [ ] Regression files committed and re-tested automatically
- [ ] Zero property violations on current codebase","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:14.993885404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:47:06.455536211Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T17:47:06.455029301Z","close_reason":"Added 8 proptest properties for layout mathematical invariants: determinism (random DAG + star), rank consistency for Sugiyama DAGs, non-overlap for random DAGs, connectivity preservation, boundedness for all coords (random DAG + star + force-directed). 64 cases each = 512 random graphs per run. Graph generators: random_dag() and star_ir().","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["invariants","methodology","property-testing","proptest"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:14.993885404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:42.022238936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-17e4.5","title":"Implement CI quality gates for alien concept adoption","description":"## CI Quality Gates for Alien Concept Adoption

Implement CI pipeline gates that enforce the graveyard methodology: no alien concept integration merges without evidence, no performance regression without justification, no golden checksum changes without approval.

## Gate Definitions

**Gate 1: Golden Checksum Guard**
- Trigger: any change to fm-layout or fm-core
- Check: golden checksums unchanged, OR commit message contains [golden-update] with justification
- Fail action: block merge, link to golden checksum update workflow

**Gate 2: Performance Regression Guard**
- Trigger: any change to fm-layout, fm-core, or fm-parser
- Check: benchmark suite runs, all metrics within 10% of baseline (configurable threshold)
- Fail action: block merge if any metric regresses > 10%, warn if > 5%
- Exception: commit message contains [perf-regression-ok: reason]

**Gate 3: Property Test Guard**
- Trigger: any change to fm-layout, fm-core, fm-parser
- Check: all proptest properties pass with 10000 cases
- Fail action: block merge on any property violation

**Gate 4: Evidence Ledger Guard**
- Trigger: any bead with label "alien-cs" moved to complete status
- Check: corresponding evidence ledger entry exists with post-metrics
- Fail action: warn (not block), link to evidence creation workflow

**Gate 5: Determinism Guard**
- Trigger: any change to fm-layout
- Check: run layout 10 times on corpus, all outputs identical
- Fail action: block merge on non-determinism

**Gate 6: Decision Contract Guard**
- Trigger: any alien-cs epic moved to complete
- Check: decision contract document exists in evidence/ directory
- Fail action: warn (not block)

## Implementation
1. Define gates as GitHub Actions workflow steps (or equivalent CI).
2. Each gate is a separate job for parallel execution.
3. Gate results aggregated into a single merge-blocking status check.
4. Override mechanism: repo admin can force-merge with audit trail.
5. Gate configuration stored in `.ci/quality-gates.toml` (thresholds, exceptions).
6. Dashboard: CI generates a quality summary comment on each PR.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 gates implemented and running in CI
- [ ] Gates block merge on failure (except Gate 4 and 6 which warn)
- [ ] Override mechanism exists with audit trail
- [ ] Configuration is in-repo and version-controlled
- [ ] Quality summary comment posted on each PR
- [ ] Gate execution time < 10 minutes total (parallel execution)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:30.166665414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:21:07.394623345Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","ci","methodology","quality-gates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:30.166665414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:42.133640448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:42.245035750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-17e4.6","title":"Create decision contract template and initial contracts for all alien concepts","description":"## Decision Contract Template and Initial Contracts

Create a formal decision contract template for alien CS concept adoption, and draft initial contracts for all alien concepts being considered for FrankenMermaid.

## Decision Contract Template
Each alien concept adoption must have a decision contract BEFORE implementation begins. The contract specifies:

```markdown
# Decision Contract: [Concept Name] (§X.Y)

## Graveyard Score: X.X / Tier: A|B|C

## Hypothesis
[What improvement do we expect? How much? On what workloads?]

## Acceptance Criteria (Adopt)
- [ ] Metric A improved by >= X% on benchmark corpus
- [ ] No metric regressed by > Y%
- [ ] Implementation complexity <= Z LOC
- [ ] Evidence ledger entry complete with measurements

## Rejection Criteria (Reject)
- [ ] Metric A improvement < X% (threshold for "not worth it")
- [ ] Any metric regression > Y%
- [ ] Implementation complexity > Z LOC
- [ ] Solver/algorithm timeout > T ms on target workload

## Evaluation Protocol
1. Baseline measurement (pre-implementation)
2. Implementation
3. Post-measurement (same corpus, same metrics)
4. Statistical comparison with confidence intervals
5. Decision: Adopt / Reject / Defer / Hybrid

## Timeline
- Baseline: [date]
- Implementation: [date range]
- Evaluation: [date]
- Decision: [date]

## Reviewers
[Who reviews and ratifies the decision?]
```

## Initial Contracts to Draft
1. **E-Graphs for Crossing Minimization (§6.6)** — Score 3.0, Tier B
   - Accept if: >= 15% crossing reduction on dense graphs, fallback < 100ms
   - Reject if: < 5% improvement or fallback > 500ms

2. **Swiss Tables for Node/Edge Maps (§7.7)** — Score 3.0, Tier B
   - Accept if: >= 20% lookup throughput improvement, >= 5% end-to-end
   - Reject if: < 5% end-to-end improvement (already using hashbrown)

3. **Conformal Geometric Algebra (§12.11)** — Score 2.5, Tier C
   - Accept if: code clarity measurably improved, performance within 2x of matrices
   - Reject if: performance > 3x of matrices on rendering hot path

4. **Constraint Programming for Layout (§9.7)** — Score 3.5, Tier B
   - Accept if: constraints respected, solve time < 1s for 200 nodes
   - Reject if: solve time > 5s for 100 nodes

5. **Bidirectional Lenses (§6.2)** — Score 4.0, Tier B
   - Accept if: GetPut and PutGet laws hold for 100% of corpus
   - Reject if: round-trip latency > 200ms on 500-node diagram

6. **Incremental Subgraph Re-Layout (§6.1)** — Score 6.7, Tier A
   - Mandatory adoption. Accept if: < 10ms update on 1000-node diagram
   - Remediation required if: > 50ms or non-deterministic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decision contract template created in evidence/TEMPLATE.md
- [ ] Initial contracts drafted for all 6 alien concepts above
- [ ] Each contract has specific, measurable accept/reject criteria
- [ ] Contracts reviewed and ratified before implementation begins
- [ ] Contracts version-controlled in evidence/contracts/ directory","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:48.635623903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:41:13.427956875Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:41:13.427458842Z","close_reason":"Created decision contract template (evidence/TEMPLATE.md) and 6 initial contracts: E-Graphs (§6.6), Swiss Tables (§7.7), CGA (§12.11), Constraint Programming (§9.7), Bidirectional Lenses (§6.2), Incremental Re-Layout (§6.1). Each has specific measurable accept/reject criteria, evaluation protocols, and benchmark corpus definitions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","decision-contract","documentation","methodology"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:48.635623903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-18eq","title":"Leapfrog Triejoin for multi-relation DSL queries","description":"Worst-case optimal joins via Leapfrog Triejoin (graveyard section 8.1) for complex multi-relation queries in diagram DSL. Relevant for diagram query/filter operations.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:05.132825089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:05.132825089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","frankenmermaid","graph-ir"]}
 {"id":"bd-1a45","title":"C4: Implement technology labels, element icons, and legend rendering","description":"# C4 Diagram Full Rendering

## What mermaid-js Supports
C4 diagrams have rich element types with icons, descriptions, and technology labels:
- Person: stick figure icon
- System/Container/Component: rectangles with type label, name, technology, description
- External systems: dashed borders
- Databases: cylinder shape
- Queues: parallelogram shape
- Boundaries: labeled dashed rectangles
- Relationships: arrows with labels and technology annotations
- Legend: auto-generated showing element type → visual mapping
- LAYOUT_TOP_DOWN() / LAYOUT_LEFT_RIGHT() directives

## Current State
- All element types ARE parsed
- All relationship types ARE parsed
- Boundaries ARE parsed
- Technology/description fields within function args are NOT fully extracted (only first arg parsed)
- Element type icons NOT rendered
- Legend NOT rendered
- Layout directives parsed but NOT applied

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse all positional arguments in C4 function calls: (id, name, [technology], [description]). Store technology and description on IrNode.
2. **SVG Renderer**: Render element type labels (<<Person>>, <<System>>, etc.). Show technology in brackets below name. Show description in smaller text. Render person as stick figure. Render databases as cylinders. Render external elements with dashed borders.
3. **Layout**: Honor LAYOUT_TOP_DOWN/LEFT_RIGHT by setting graph direction.
4. **Legend**: Generate a legend box showing element type mapping.
5. **Tests**: Full C4 context diagram, container diagram, component diagram with all element types.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:12.054865942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.346483380Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c4","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":169,"issue_id":"bd-1a45","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:43Z"},{"id":189,"issue_id":"bd-1a45","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. C4Context with Person, System, System_Ext → verify person stick figure icon, system rectangle, ext dashed border
2. Container with technology 'Java Spring' → verify technology renders in brackets below name
3. Rel with technology 'REST/HTTPS' → verify technology on relationship label
4. Container_Boundary with nested components → verify boundary dashed rectangle with title
5. LAYOUT_TOP_DOWN() → verify direction changes to TB
6. Legend → verify auto-generated legend box showing element type mapping
7. SystemDb → verify cylinder shape for database system
8. Full C4 deployment diagram with Deployment_Node nesting → verify nested boundaries","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+[golden_checksum]
+enabled = true
+blocking = true
+layout_paths = ["crates/fm-layout/**", "crates/fm-core/**"]
+svg_bless_env = "BLESS"
+layout_bless_env = "BLESS_LAYOUT"
+allow_commit_tag = "[golden-update]"
+
+[performance_regression]
+enabled = true
+blocking = true
+paths = ["crates/fm-layout/**", "crates/fm-core/**", "crates/fm-parser/**"]
+warn_threshold_pct = 5.0
+fail_threshold_pct = 10.0
+allow_commit_tag_prefix = "[perf-regression-ok:"
+benchmark_command = "cargo test -p fm-layout benchmark -- --nocapture"
+
+[property_test]
+enabled = true
+blocking = true
+cases = 10000
+command = "cargo test --workspace --all-targets"
+
+[evidence_ledger]
+enabled = true
+blocking = false
+labels = ["alien-cs"]
+evidence_dir = "evidence"
+contracts_dir = "evidence/contracts"
+
+[determinism]
+enabled = true
+blocking = true
+runs = 10
+layout_paths = ["crates/fm-layout/**"]

+          }

+  golden-checksum-guard:
+    name: Golden Checksum Guard
+    needs: quality-gate-config
+    if: needs.quality-gate-config.outputs.golden_enabled == 'true'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Setup Rust Toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+
+      - name: Cache Cargo Artifacts
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+
+      - name: SVG golden guard
+        run: cargo test -p fm-cli --test golden_svg_test -- --nocapture
+
+      - name: Layout golden guard
+        run: cargo test -p fm-cli --test golden_layout_test -- --nocapture
+
+  property-test-guard:
+    name: Property Test Guard
+    needs: quality-gate-config
+    if: needs.quality-gate-config.outputs.property_enabled == 'true'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    env:
+      PROPTEST_CASES: ${{ needs.quality-gate-config.outputs.proptest_cases }}
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Setup Rust Toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+
+      - name: Cache Cargo Artifacts
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+
+      - name: Run property-heavy workspace tests
+        run: cargo test --workspace --all-targets -- --nocapture
+
+  determinism-guard:
+    name: Determinism Guard
+    needs: quality-gate-config
+    if: needs.quality-gate-config.outputs.determinism_enabled == 'true'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Setup Rust Toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+
+      - name: Cache Cargo Artifacts
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+
+      - name: Run determinism guard
+        run: ${{ needs.quality-gate-config.outputs.determinism_command }}
+
+      - name: Run pipeline determinism smoke
+        run: cargo test -p fm-cli --test integration_test e2e_pipeline_determinism_all_types -- --nocapture
+
+  performance-regression-guard:
+    name: Performance Regression Guard
+    needs: quality-gate-config
+    if: needs.quality-gate-config.outputs.performance_enabled == 'true'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Setup Rust Toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+
+      - name: Cache Cargo Artifacts
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+
+      - name: Run benchmark evidence command
+        run: ${{ needs.quality-gate-config.outputs.benchmark_command }}
+
+  evidence-ledger-guard:
+    name: Evidence Ledger Guard
+    needs: quality-gate-config
+    if: needs.quality-gate-config.outputs.evidence_enabled == 'true'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    continue-on-error: true
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Verify evidence corpus exists
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          test -d evidence
+          test -f evidence/TEMPLATE.md
+          test -d evidence/contracts
+          find evidence/contracts -maxdepth 1 -name '*.md' | grep -q .
+
+  decision-contract-guard:
+    name: Decision Contract Guard
+    needs: quality-gate-config
+    if: needs.quality-gate-config.outputs.decision_enabled == 'true'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    continue-on-error: true
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Verify decision contracts exist
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          find evidence/contracts -maxdepth 1 -name '*.md' | grep -q .
+
+  quality-summary:
+    name: Quality Summary
+    needs:
+      - quality-gate-config
+      - core-check
+      - golden-checksum-guard
+      - property-test-guard
+      - determinism-guard
+      - performance-regression-guard
+      - evidence-ledger-guard
+      - decision-contract-guard
+      - wasm-build
+      - coverage
+    if: always()
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - name: Summarize gate results
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          {
+            echo "## Quality Gates"
+            echo ""
+            echo "| Gate | Result |"
+            echo "| --- | --- |"
+            echo "| core-check | ${{ needs.core-check.result }} |"
+            echo "| golden-checksum-guard | ${{ needs.golden-checksum-guard.result }} |"
+            echo "| property-test-guard | ${{ needs.property-test-guard.result }} |"
+            echo "| determinism-guard | ${{ needs.determinism-guard.result }} |"
+            echo "| performance-regression-guard | ${{ needs.performance-regression-guard.result }} |"
+            echo "| evidence-ledger-guard | ${{ needs.evidence-ledger-guard.result }} |"
+            echo "| decision-contract-guard | ${{ needs.decision-contract-guard.result }} |"
+            echo "| wasm-build | ${{ needs.wasm-build.result }} |"
+            echo "| coverage | ${{ needs.coverage.result }} |"
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+
+      - name: Enforce blocking gate status
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          failures=0
+          for result in \
+            "${{ needs.core-check.result }}" \
+            "${{ needs.golden-checksum-guard.result }}" \
+            "${{ needs.property-test-guard.result }}" \
+            "${{ needs.determinism-guard.result }}" \
+            "${{ needs.performance-regression-guard.result }}" \
+            "${{ needs.wasm-build.result }}" \
+            "${{ needs.coverage.result }}"; do
+            if [ "$result" != "success" ]; then
+              failures=1
+            fi
+          done
+          exit "$failures"
+
   wasm-build:
     name: WASM Build
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
